questions.text: The fluid part of the blood is called:
questions.explanation: Plasma is the liquid component of blood in which cells are suspended.

questions.text: The red pigment present in Red Blood Cells (RBCs) is:
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin binds with oxygen and transports it to all parts of the body.

questions.text: Which blood cells fight against germs that may enter our body?
questions.explanation: White Blood Cells (WBC) are part of the body's immune system.

questions.text: The formation of a clot at the site of injury is due to:
questions.explanation: Platelets are responsible for blood clotting to prevent excessive bleeding.

questions.text: Which blood vessels carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body?
questions.explanation: Arteries carry oxygenated blood away from the heart (except the pulmonary artery).

questions.text: The throbbing movement in the wrist is called:
questions.explanation: Pulse is due to blood flowing in the arteries after being pumped by the heart.

questions.text: The number of pulse beats per minute is called:
questions.explanation: A resting person usually has a pulse rate between 72 and 80 beats per minute.

questions.text: Which blood vessels carry carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the heart?
questions.explanation: Veins carry deoxygenated blood from the tissues back to the heart.

questions.text: Veins have ________ to ensure blood flows only towards the heart.
questions.explanation: Valves prevent the backflow of blood in the veins.

questions.text: The extremely thin tubes where arteries and veins join are:
questions.explanation: Capillaries allow for the exchange of materials between blood and tissues.

questions.text: Which artery carries carbon dioxide-rich blood to the lungs?
questions.explanation: The pulmonary artery is an exception; it carries deoxygenated blood.

questions.text: Which vein carries oxygen-rich blood to the heart?
questions.explanation: The pulmonary vein is an exception; it carries oxygenated blood from lungs to heart.

questions.text: The heart is located in the ________ cavity.
questions.explanation: The heart is situated in the chest cavity, tilted slightly towards the left.

questions.text: How many chambers does the human heart have?
questions.explanation: The heart has two upper chambers (atria) and two lower chambers (ventricles).

questions.text: The instrument used by doctors to feel heartbeats is:
questions.explanation: A stethoscope amplifies the sound of heartbeats.

questions.text: The rhythmic contraction and relaxation of heart muscles is called:
questions.explanation: Heartbeat is felt throughout the life of an organism.

questions.text: Organisms like sponges and Hydra do not have a circulatory system.
questions.explanation: They use water to transport food and oxygen and remove waste products.

questions.text: The process of removal of waste products produced in cells is called:
questions.explanation: Excretion involves filtering out harmful waste from the blood.

questions.text: The human excretory system consists of:
questions.explanation: This system filters blood and removes urea and salts as urine.

questions.text: Urine consists of 95% water and:
questions.explanation: Urine primarily removes nitrogenous waste like urea from the body.

questions.text: The artificial filtering of blood when kidneys fail is called:
questions.explanation: Dialysis is used to remove wastes from the blood when kidneys are not functional.

questions.text: Sweat contains water and:
questions.explanation: Sweat helps in cooling the body and removing excess salts.

questions.text: The vascular tissue for the transport of water and minerals in plants is:
questions.explanation: Xylem forms a continuous network of channels from roots to leaves.

questions.text: The vascular tissue for the transport of food in plants is:
questions.explanation: Phloem transports food prepared in the leaves to all other parts of the plant.

questions.text: The loss of water in the form of vapor through stomata is called:
questions.explanation: Transpiration creates a suction pull that helps in pulling water up to great heights.

questions.text: Root hairs increase the ________ for absorption of water and minerals.
questions.explanation: More surface area allows for faster absorption of water from the soil.

questions.text: Aquatic animals like fish excrete cell waste as ________ which dissolves in water.
questions.explanation: Ammonia is the primary waste product in many aquatic animals.

questions.text: Birds and lizards excrete a semi-solid, white colored compound called:
questions.explanation: Uric acid excretion is an adaptation to conserve water in these animals.

questions.text: Why do we feel cool after sweating?
questions.explanation: Evaporation of water from the skin surface absorbs heat from the body.

questions.text: Which of these is responsible for the suction pull in tall trees?
questions.explanation: The evaporation of water from leaves creates a tension that pulls water upward through xylem.

questions.text: In the human heart, the left side handles ________ blood.
questions.explanation: The left atrium and ventricle deal with oxygenated blood coming from the lungs.

questions.text: The right side of the human heart handles ________ blood.
questions.explanation: The right side receives deoxygenated blood from the body and sends it to the lungs.

questions.text: Which blood cell has no nucleus when mature (in humans)?
questions.explanation: The absence of a nucleus provides more space for hemoglobin and oxygen in RBCs.

questions.text: William Harvey is famous for discovering:
questions.explanation: English physician William Harvey described the systemic circulation and properties of blood.

questions.text: The muscular partition that separates the heart chambers is the:
questions.explanation: The septum prevents the mixing of oxygen-rich and carbon dioxide-rich blood.

questions.text: Which artery is the largest in the human body?
questions.explanation: The aorta carries oxygenated blood from the left ventricle to the rest of the body.

questions.text: The tube-like structures that transport water to leaves in plants are made of ________ cells.
questions.explanation: Vascular tissue forms the piping system for transport in plants.

questions.text: Blood is ________ in color due to the presence of iron-containing hemoglobin.
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin contains iron which gives blood its characteristic red color.

questions.text: What is the primary function of Phloem?
questions.explanation: Phloem carries glucose from leaves to roots, stems, and fruits.

questions.text: Which organ filters the blood to form urine?
questions.explanation: Kidneys contain millions of tiny filtration units called nephrons.
